# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: Custom User Authentication
Description: A custom WordPress plugin for user registration and login with password hashing.
Version: 1.0
Author: Your Name
*/

function custom_auth_register() {
    if (isset($_POST['custom_register'])) {
        $username = sanitize_user($_POST['username']);
        $password = sanitize_text_field($_POST['password']);
        $email = sanitize_email($_POST['email']);
        
        if (!username_exists($username) && !email_exists($email)) {
            $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
            $user_id = wp_create_user($username, $hashed_password, $email);
            if (!is_wp_error($user_id)) {
                echo "User registered successfully.";
            } else {
                echo "Error: " . $user_id->get_error_message();
            }
        } else {
            echo "Username or Email already exists.";
        }
    }
}

function custom_auth_login() {
    if (isset($_POST['custom_login'])) {
        $username = sanitize_user($_POST['username']);
        $password = sanitize_text_field($_POST['password']);
        
        $user = get_user_by('login', $username);
        if ($user && password_verify($password, $user->user_pass)) {
            wp_set_current_user($user->ID);
            wp_set_auth_cookie($user->ID);
            echo "Login successful.";
        } else {
            echo "Invalid username or password.";
        }
    }
}

function custom_auth_register_form() {
    ?>
    <form method="POST">
        <input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Username" required>
        <input type="email" name="email" placeholder="Email" required>
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" required>
        <input type="submit" name="custom_register" value="Register">
    </form>
    <?php
}

function custom_auth_login_form() {
    ?>
    <form method="POST">
        <input type="text" name="username" placeholder="Username" required>
        <input type="password" name="password" placeholder="Password" required>
        <input type="submit" name="custom_login" value="Login">
    </form>
    <?php
}

add_shortcode('custom_auth_register_form', 'custom_auth_register_form');
add_shortcode('custom_auth_login_form', 'custom_auth_login_form');

add_action('init', 'custom_auth_register');
add_action('init', 'custom_auth_login');
?>
